# shared { #app.shared }

`shared`

Data access layer for the BAM dashboard backend.

Orchestrates data flow between remote data servers, the TiTiler mapping 
gateway, and local disk-backed fallback caches for rasters and tabular files.

## Main Capabilities {.doc-section .doc-section-main-capabilities}

* Monitors the dynamic cloud raster tiler API health status.
* Resolves HTTP URLs for remote Cloud Optimized GeoTIFFs (COGs).
* Extracts and aggregates data tables efficiently using Polars.
* Parses Apache directory listings to scan for available assets.

## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

All tabular data workflows utilize the high-performance `polars` engine.

## Functions

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [available_regions](#app.shared.available_regions) | Identify geographic regions for a specific species and  |
| [available_years](#app.shared.available_years) | Parse available model years for a specific species and region by scanning filenames. |
| [get_cov_fx_data](#app.shared.get_cov_fx_data) | Extract and assemble regional marginal effect datasets across target covariates. |
| [get_tif_path](#app.shared.get_tif_path) | Construct the remote HTTP target URL path to a Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF from filter inputs. |
| [list_directory](#app.shared.list_directory) | Scrape and extract valid paths from DRAC server directory listing. |
| [load_abundance_data](#app.shared.load_abundance_data) | Load population density estimates from the Excel results summary file. |
| [load_covariate_metadata](#app.shared.load_covariate_metadata) | Load Covariate metadata and full-form names from the csv file. |
| [load_region_data](#app.shared.load_region_data) | Load region data from the Excel results summary file. |
| [load_species_metadata](#app.shared.load_species_metadata) | Load species taxonomic and descriptive data from the Excel results summary file. |
| [read_md](#app.shared.read_md) | Extract raw text contents out of a localized target Markdown document. |
| [read_yaml](#app.shared.read_yaml) | Load and parse a localized target YAML configuration layout file. |
| [species_ids](#app.shared.species_ids) | Scan the remote repository index to isolate available species IDs. |
| [tiler_is_healthy](#app.shared.tiler_is_healthy) | Check the health status of the remote TiTiler API gateway. |
| [url_exists](#app.shared.url_exists) | Verify the availability of a specific asset URL on the DRAC server. |

### available_regions { #app.shared.available_regions }

```python
shared.available_regions(species_id)
```

Identify geographic regions for a specific species and 
list available regions from remote directory.

Falling back to local directories if the remote server is offline.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name       | Type   | Description                            | Default    |
|------------|--------|----------------------------------------|------------|
| species_id | str    | The unique identifier for the species. | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type        | Description                                                      |
|--------|-------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | list of str | Sorted list of region names found within the species' directory. |

### available_years { #app.shared.available_years }

```python
shared.available_years(species_id, region)
```

Parse available model years for a specific species and region by scanning filenames.

This function looks for .tif files following the naming convention 
'{species}_{region}_{year}.tif' and extracts the integer year.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name       | Type   | Description                            | Default    |
|------------|--------|----------------------------------------|------------|
| species_id | str    | The unique identifier for the species. | _required_ |
| region     | str    | The geographic region identifier.      | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type        | Description                                          |
|--------|-------------|------------------------------------------------------|
|        | list of int | Sorted list of years found for the given parameters. |

### get_cov_fx_data { #app.shared.get_cov_fx_data }

```python
shared.get_cov_fx_data(covs)
```

Extract and assemble regional marginal effect datasets across target covariates.

Reads separate flat tables across multiple parameter directories and combines
them into a unified structural representation.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type        | Description                                                      | Default    |
|--------|-------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| covs   | list of str | The covariate variable collection subdirectory names to extract. | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type         | Description                                                                   |
|--------|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | pl.DataFrame | A Polars DataFrame containing combined parameters for all selected variables. |

### get_tif_path { #app.shared.get_tif_path }

```python
shared.get_tif_path(species_id, region, year)
```

Construct the remote HTTP target URL path to a Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF from filter inputs.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name       | Type   | Description                                                             | Default    |
|------------|--------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| species_id | str    | The unique alpha-numeric lookup identifier code for the species.        | _required_ |
| region     | str    | The regional sub-boundary identifier string (e.g., 'Canada', 'Alaska'). | _required_ |
| year       | int    | The target modeling year parameter.                                     | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description                                                                 |
|--------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | str    | The absolute concatenated URL address location pointing to the raster file. |

### list_directory { #app.shared.list_directory }

```python
shared.list_directory(url)
```

Scrape and extract valid paths from DRAC server directory listing.

Requests index endpoints and ignores structural navigation entries, 
query filter strings, or top-level relative assets.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type   | Description                                           | Default    |
|--------|--------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| url    | str    | The remote directory link listing resource locations. | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type        | Description                                                              |
|--------|-------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | list of str | Cleaned asset filenames or subdirectory names discovered in the catalog. |

### load_abundance_data { #app.shared.load_abundance_data }

```python
shared.load_abundance_data()
```

Load population density estimates from the Excel results summary file.

- species specific population size (million males) and density estimates (males / ha) by region (density = abundance / area)
    - id:                   AOU code for the species
    - scientific:           scientific name for the species
    - english:              common name for the species in English
    - region:               modelling region
    - year:                 year of prediction, determined by year of covariate prediction layers used
    - population_estimate:  mean population estimate
    - population_lower:     5th percentile of 32 bootstrap population estimate
    - population_upper:     95th percentile of 32 bootstrap population estimate
    - density_estimate:     mean density estimate
    - density_lower:        5th percentile of 32 bootstrap density estimate
    - density_upper:        5th percentile of 32 bootstrap density estimate

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type         | Description                                                    |
|--------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | pl.DataFrame | A Polars DataFrame containing the 'abundances' sheet metadata. |

### load_covariate_metadata { #app.shared.load_covariate_metadata }

```python
shared.load_covariate_metadata()
```

Load Covariate metadata and full-form names from the csv file.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type         | Description                                           |
|--------|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
|        | pl.DataFrame | A Polars DataFrame containing the Covariate metadata. |

### load_region_data { #app.shared.load_region_data }

```python
shared.load_region_data()
```

Load region data from the Excel results summary file.

- lists the individual modelling regions and the mosaiced model regions
    - region:               code for the region (country and bird conservation region number for single model regions)
    - type:                 type of model region (single model vs mosaic of multiple single models)
    - country:              country of the region
    - bcr:                  Bird conservation region (BCR) name
    - area_km2:             area of the region (km2)
    - total_surveys:        total surveys available for the modelling region (including 100km buffer)
    - bootstrap_surveys:    total surveys used for model training per bootsstrap (including 100 km buffer)

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type         | Description                                             |
|--------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
|        | pl.DataFrame | A Polars DataFrame containing the 'regions' sheet data. |

### load_species_metadata { #app.shared.load_species_metadata }

```python
shared.load_species_metadata()
```

Load species taxonomic and descriptive data from the Excel results summary file.

- lists the species for which results are available
    - id:                   AOU code for the species
    - scientific:           scientific name for the species
    - english:              common name for the species in English
    - french:               common name for the species in French
    - family:               family for the species

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type         | Description                                                 |
|--------|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | pl.DataFrame | A Polars DataFrame containing the 'species' sheet metadata. |

### read_md { #app.shared.read_md }

```python
shared.read_md(filename)
```

Extract raw text contents out of a localized target Markdown document.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name     | Type   | Description                                             | Default    |
|----------|--------|---------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| filename | str    | The name of the target file stored under `CONTENT_DIR`. | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description                                              |
|--------|--------|----------------------------------------------------------|
|        | str    | The unparsed text content block encoded via UTF-8 rules. |

### read_yaml { #app.shared.read_yaml }

```python
shared.read_yaml(filename)
```

Load and parse a localized target YAML configuration layout file.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name     | Type   | Description                                                     | Default    |
|----------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| filename | str    | The name of the configuration asset stored under `CONTENT_DIR`. | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description                                                        |
|--------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | dict   | A dictionary representation of the parsed YAML document structure. |

### species_ids { #app.shared.species_ids }

```python
shared.species_ids()
```

Scan the remote repository index to isolate available species IDs.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type        | Description                                                                                                         |
|--------|-------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | list of str | Sorted list of species IDs based on existing subdirectories. Returns an empty list if remote catalog requests fail. |

### tiler_is_healthy { #app.shared.tiler_is_healthy }

```python
shared.tiler_is_healthy()
```

Check the health status of the remote TiTiler API gateway.

Uses a basic Time-To-Live (TTL) caching mechanism to prevent spamming
the health endpoint on rapid UI reactive invalidations.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description                                                                                                |
|--------|--------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | bool   | True if the tiler endpoint responds with a status code of 200 and an 'ok' status payload, False otherwise. |

#### Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

The cache state is managed globally by the `_tiler_health_cache` dictionary
using the window duration defined in `TILER_HEALTH_TTL`.

### url_exists { #app.shared.url_exists }

```python
shared.url_exists(url)
```

Verify the availability of a specific asset URL on the DRAC server.

Performs a lightweight HEAD request to verify file existence without
downloading the payload.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type   | Description                      | Default    |
|--------|--------|----------------------------------|------------|
| url    | str    | The full URL string to evaluate. | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description                                                        |
|--------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | bool   | True if the server responds with status code 200, False otherwise. |
